axis_responsibility.agent_axis: Agent owns only received_work and returned once S3 validates and consumes the submitted payload; the claim token is not Agent identity.
axis_responsibility.brick_axis: Brick owns the parked step work contract, required_return_shape, and graph Building Plan structure.
axis_responsibility.evidence_surface: Claim/submission/release files are support evidence and checker inputs, not source truth or quality judgment.
axis_responsibility.link_axis: Link owns declared Movement and gate sufficiency after S3 re-entry; claim, submit, and release do not choose route or route destination.
axis_responsibility.rejected_shortcut: Do not treat generic human/COO disposition resume, codex exec resume, or adapter identity as the S3 authority.
axis_responsibility.support_surface: Support owns atomic filesystem claim mechanics, passive submission files, validation, replay/injection mechanics, and evidence recording.

candidate_file_changes: support/operator/run.py - add public claim, submit, release functions or thin exports; replace parked resume early reject with S3 admission branch; add linear/single-step chat-session graph-law rejection.

support/operator/walker_resume.py - read parked envelope, active claim, release records, submission, declared graph birth certificate, completed step outputs, and recorded returns; perform S3 door validation before write.
support/operator/walker_kernel.py - add a support-only parked-submission seed path that replays completed steps and injects the submitted parked return while computing the parked gate live.
support/recording/contracts.py - add support-only record contracts for chat-session claim, submission, and release if record shapes need checker-derived field specs.
support/recording/step_outputs.py - add path helpers and writers for chat-session claim/submission/release files next to work-envelope.json.

support/checkers/check_building_lifecycle_path_shape.py - admit and validate claim/submission/release path shapes, token form, no session text, no forbidden authority fields, and resumed parked step-output coexistence.
support/checkers/check_package_path_admission.py - admit the same new work/step-outputs chat-session claim/submission/release path shapes.
support/checkers/lib/kernel_checks.py - add chat_session_submit_resume_seam temp drive plus FIRE cases; update S1 linear-positive case to graph-law negative.
support/checkers/profiles/building_automation.yaml - add pins for the new checker and support functions.
support/operator/evidence_status.py and support/operator/frontier_observation.py - optionally observe claimed/submitted parked support files without changing source-truth or Link authority.
checker_or_verifier_plan: Checker-first: add a new chat_session_submit_resume_seam kernel check and wire it into the building_automation profile before implementation.

Atomicity FIRE: drive two concurrent claim attempts against the same parked step; exactly one creates claim-0001.json and the loser names that claim. Mutating the claim path to non-exclusive creation must go RED.
Token FIRE: force the token minter to return UUID-shaped and ULID-shaped text; checker must go RED before any evidence write.

Door FIRE: invalid submissions with forbidden keys, UUID/ULID text, missing token, mismatched token, absent claim, and absent submission must reject loudly and leave parked evidence without raw/agent-return.jsonl or step-output.json.

Resume FIRE: valid graph temp drive parks, claims, rejects second claim, submits, resumes, replays completed prior steps from record, consumes the submission as the parked step return, computes gates, runs the next live step, and reaches the declared next frontier or close.
Graph-law FIRE: linear plan plus adapter:chat-session must reject at admission; the existing S1 checker linear-positive case must become a negative case.

Path FIRE: generated claim/submission/release paths must pass both package path admission and lifecycle path shape; a mutated generated tree with missing or malformed claim/submission path must go RED.
Full gate after implementation: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 PYTHONPATH=support/import_identity python3 support/checkers/check_profile.py --all.
returned.design_summary: Implement S2/S3 as support-only claim, submit, and parked-resume mechanics. Claim and submit stay passive support records next to the S1 work envelope. S3 is the only engine re-entry: it validates active claim plus matching submission before any new evidence write, injects the submitted returned payload as the parked step return, replays completed prior steps from record, computes gates at re-entry, and then continues the declared graph walk. No Link vocabulary, AgentFact shape, provider runtime, scheduler, queue, retry, timer, or quality/verdict surface should be added.
edge_cases: Two simultaneous claims for the same parked envelope.
Existing active claim with no release.
Claim released by human/COO and then reclaimed with the next generation.
Claim exists but submission is absent.
Submission exists but token is missing or mismatched.
Submission attempts to include status, success, result, target_ref, movement, credential, session_id, or other forbidden returned keys.
Submission contains UUID/ULID-shaped text in returned data or claim token.
Parked step is the first step and there are no prior completed steps to replay.
Prior completed step has a gate_sequence_policy but its recorded gate decision is missing.
Graph fan-in/fan-out after the parked step must continue through existing dynamic walker behavior.
Linear plan or run_building_once with adapter:chat-session must reject before evidence write.
Rejected S3 attempt must not fabricate raw/agent-return.jsonl or step-output.json.

invariants: Claim is exclusive by atomic filesystem primitive.
Claim token is word-form only and never UUID/ULID-shaped.
Submission is passive and cannot trigger run, resume, gate computation, or report delivery.
S3 validates before any engine evidence write.
Forbidden returned keys are rejected verbatim and never stripped.
Session-like text is rejected before evidence writes.
Completed steps before the park are replayed from record and not re-invoked.
The parked step consumes exactly the validated submission as its returned payload.
Gates compute only at S3 re-entry and through existing gate seams.
A chat-session adapter step is admitted only in graph plans.
New file shapes must be admitted in both path checkers.
All proof claims require executed temp-drive and negative-control evidence.

observed_evidence: Read-only inspection only; no files were edited, created, deleted, committed, pushed, or checked out.
The current S1 park seam is present: adapter:chat-session is admitted, raises a typed AgentAdapterParked signal, and run surfaces write parked frontier evidence before AgentFact creation.
The current generic resume surface explicitly rejects chat_session_parked frontiers with the S2/S3 submit/claim reason; that is the exact S3 branch to replace.

The existing dynamic resume model replays already-recorded Agent returns and recorded gate decisions; a parked step has no AgentFact yet, so S3 needs a parked-submission seed rather than pretending the parked step was already completed.
Current S1 checker still drives both linear and dynamic chat-session park cases; the new graph-plan law intentionally changes that expectation and needs a RED-first checker update.
The active declared Building for this work is a graph plan and its implementation write scope is limited to support/* and brick/building_plans/*.

proposed_changes: Add operator-callable support functions for claim, submit, and human/COO release. Keep them synchronous functions only; no CLI, server, polling, scheduler, queue, retry, or timer.

Use append-only generational file shapes next to the parked envelope, for example work/step-outputs/<step-attempt>/chat-session-claims/claim-0001.json, release-0001.json, and chat-session-submissions/submission-0001.json. Claim creation uses O_CREAT plus O_EXCL on the next claim index; concurrent claimants choose the same next index, so exactly one wins and the loser names the existing claim.
Mint claim tokens as lowercase hyphenated word tuples such as amber-bridge-calm-lantern. Add a token-form validator and self-test that rejects UUID-shaped and ULID-shaped tokens.

Make submit a passive file write only. The submission file may carry only claim_ref, claim_token, returned, proof_limits, and not_proven. It must not compute gates, call run/resume, write raw/agent-return.jsonl, or alter Link records.

Make claim release human/COO-disposition-only: release records require author_ref with human: or coo: prefix and reason_refs. Release frees the slot by making the next claim generation eligible; it does not choose Link route or compute gates.

Replace the parked early reject in resume_building_plan with a parked-resume admission branch. Missing claim, released claim only, missing submission, token mismatch, malformed token, forbidden returned key, UUID/ULID text, or shape mismatch must raise loudly and leave the parked Building evidence otherwise unchanged.

Add a support-only ParkedSubmissionSeed or equivalent in walker_kernel. It should replay completed pre-park steps from recorded step outputs and gate records, inject the validated submission as the parked step's first return without provider invocation, compute that parked step's gate live at S3 re-entry, then continue the existing graph loop.
Enforce graph-plan law at admission: any run surface receiving adapter:chat-session in a linear plan or single-step fixture rejects before parking. The dynamic graph path remains admitted.

Keep direct session submission uncompromising: reject RETURNED_FORBIDDEN_KEYS verbatim, reject extra top-level returned keys outside the Brick required_return_shape unless an admitted waiver is explicitly present, validate transition_concern_evidence if declared, and do not strip or launder payloads.
Project claim/submission observations only as support evidence if needed; do not make them source truth, Link authority, or Agent self-classification.
relevant_current_structure: support/connection/agent_adapter.py owns adapter admission and typed parking; adapter:chat-session is read-capable only and raises AgentAdapterParked instead of invoking a provider.

support/recording/adapter_error_frontier.py currently contains both adapter-error and chat-session park frontier writers; the chat-session writer builds the work envelope, parked observation, lifecycle packet, building map packet, step-output files, raw park stream, and claim traces.
support/recording/step_outputs.py currently writes work-envelope.json and parked.json in work/step-outputs/<step>-attempt-N/.
support/operator/run.py owns run_building_plan, run_building_once, and resume_building_plan; resume_building_plan currently fails closed for chat_session_parked.
support/operator/walker_resume.py owns generic held dynamic graph resume by reading the written graph plan, completed step outputs, recorded Agent returns, gate records, and human/COO disposition.
support/operator/walker_kernel.py owns the dynamic graph loop and currently supports ResumeSeed for prior completed-step replay plus disposition handling.

support/checkers/check_building_lifecycle_path_shape.py and support/checkers/check_package_path_admission.py both admit and validate existing parked paths; new claim/submission/release paths must be admitted in both.
support/checkers/lib/kernel_checks.py has run_chat_session_park_seam for S1 and explicitly says it does not prove S2 submit or S3 resume.
